title: dryopteris lanceolata var. tricholepis
intro: Dryopteris lanceolata var. tricholepis, also known as the Western wood fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The Western wood fern has been used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions, and its health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
